problem_summary: 2022 MCM Problem C (data): Given five years of daily price data for Bitcoin and gold (gold only trades on market days), start with $1,000 on 9/10/2016 and build a model that produces a daily trading strategy maximizing portfolio value by 9/10/2021, using only price history. Subtasks: (1) compute final value under the optimal strategy; (2) prove the strategy is optimal; (3) sensitivity analysis on transaction costs (BTC 2%, gold 1%); (4) write a memo to the trader. This O-award paper frames it as a forecast-then-decide-then-optimize pipeline and reports a final value of $442,697.25 (~44,270% return, ~338% annualized).
models_used: Model I — ARIMA-LSTM hybrid price forecaster: ARIMA(p,d,q) captures the linear/trend component, residuals are fed to an LSTM that captures the nonlinear component, and the two predictions are summed. One-step-ahead (one-day) walk-forward forecasting with an expanding training window. Reported RMSE 0.0342 / MAPE 0.36 for the hybrid vs ARIMA 0.7034/7.21 and LSTM 0.6183/6.25.
ARIMA standalone (linear submodel): differencing for stationarity, ACF/PACF for p,q identification, AIC/SBC for order selection.

LSTM standalone (nonlinear submodel): RNN with forget/input/output gates and cell state; MATLAB implementation with 200 hidden units, Adam optimizer, 250 epochs, piecewise learning-rate schedule (initial 0.005). Residual series modeled after phase-space reconstruction with delay time 1 and embedding order 7.

Dynamic Programming (Model: baseline strategy generator): the 5-year horizon is decomposed into 1,255 daily stages with state triplet [Cash, Gold, Bitcoin]; greedy per-day decision compares BTC ROA vs Gold ROA vs Cash ROA (return on assets minus cost) to choose buy/hold/sell.

Genetic Algorithm (Model II — strategy improvement): a chromosome of 4 genes — Gene1 trading portfolio (which of C/G/B to trade, 3-bit), Gene2 trading frequency (1/3/5/7/15/30/60 days), Gene3 buying/breakout strategy type, Gene4 stop-loss percentage (1/2/3/5/8/10%). Fitness = portfolio yield; operators = roulette-wheel selection, mating, crossover, mutation (genetic variation / strategy swapping / strategy variation); backtesting used as the evaluation step in selection.
Time-value-of-money discounting: nominal terminal value discounted to present using the 5-year US Treasury yield as risk-free rate (CF_real = CF_nominal / (1+r_f)^n).
key_techniques: Hybrid decomposition forecasting: split the series into linear (ARIMA) + nonlinear (LSTM residual) parts and recombine — a clean, defensible justification for why one model alone is insufficient in a noisy financial series.
Walk-forward / expanding-window one-step-ahead prediction (predict day i using days 1..i-1, then absorb day i) — matches the no-lookahead constraint of live trading and is the honest way to backtest.
Phase-space reconstruction (delay 1, embedding order 7) to convert the residual series into LSTM inputs.
Three-tier pipeline architecture: forecast (ARIMA-LSTM) -> baseline policy (DP) -> policy optimization (GA). The GA explicitly exists to fix documented DP weaknesses (lag, missed opportunities).
Encoding a trading strategy as a binary chromosome so heterogeneous decisions (asset set, frequency, entry rule, stop-loss) become a single searchable genome.

Two-pronged optimality argument for subtask 2: (a) change the strategy internally (swap Gene4 percentage stop-loss -> capital stop-loss, yield drops 442%->273%) and (b) change the environment (slice out bull/bear/oscillating regimes and beat buy-and-hold in each).

Transaction-cost sensitivity as four discrete commission scenarios (reduce gold only / reduce BTC only / reduce both / increase both) with mechanistic explanation of how cost changes shift trade frequency per asset.
Benchmarking the strategy against passive buy-and-hold of BTC and gold over the same window to contextualize the return.
why_it_won: "From a judge's view, the paper wins on narrative coherence and completeness rather than on rigor. It nails the three things judges reward at the structural level: (1) a clear, well-motivated pipeline where every model earns its place — ARIMA and LSTM are justified by a linear/nonlinear argument, DP gives a baseline, and the GA is introduced specifically to repair named DP shortcomings, so the reader always understands WHY the next model appears; (2) it answers all four subtasks explicitly and in order, including a genuinely creative two-axis optimality proof (perturb the strategy AND perturb the market regime) that directly addresses the hard-to-operationalize 'prove it's optimal' ask; (3) strong communication — a full pipeline flowchart, per-model diagrams, a notation table, RMSE/MAPE comparison table, regime-by-regime trade plots, and a fluent, persona-driven investor memo (with a Buffett quote on risk and patience) that reads like it was written for a real client. The combination of a fashionable hybrid deep-learning forecaster, a recognizable OR method (DP), and a metaheuristic (GA) signals breadth, and the appendix ships actual MATLAB code, which reads as reproducible effort. It looks and feels like a complete, confident, well-organized submission — which is what carries an O-award even when individual numbers are shaky."
section_highlights: "Section 4 (ARIMA-LSTM) is the strongest: it motivates the hybrid with a linear-vs-nonlinear dichotomy, walks each submodel through its own diagram, and closes with a head-to-head RMSE/MAPE table (4.4) that makes the hybrid's superiority visually obvious. Section 6 (Genetic Algorithm) is the most distinctive: the explicit 4-gene chromosome encoding (Tables 2-5) turns a vague 'optimize the strategy' task into a concrete search problem and is the paper's intellectual centerpiece. Section 7 (Effectiveness Evaluation) is the cleverest response to the prompt — the dual approach of changing Gene4 (273% vs 442%) and slicing bull/bear/oscillating regimes is a memorable, judge-pleasing way to operationalize 'optimal.' Section 9 (Investor Memo) is a communication highlight: it candidly states the risk-seeker assumption, admits the strategy doesn't trade daily and can't time exact tops/bottoms, and uses an accessible Buffett analogy. The Assumptions section (2) is also strong: it explicitly grounds 'best strategy' in a stated risk preference (extreme risk-seeker), which is a sophisticated framing that pre-empts the comparability objection."
pitfalls_or_limitations: Look-ahead / leakage red flag: the DP flowchart and the MATLAB strategy code read prediction files (biteyuce/goldyuce) and effectively decide using future/forecast returns; if the 'forecast' is too accurate the whole 44,000% return is an artifact of an unrealistically good predictor rather than a good strategy.

Implausibly good forecast metrics: hybrid RMSE 0.0342 / MAPE 0.36 (and MAPE 7.21 for ARIMA alone) are suspiciously low for daily Bitcoin and almost certainly reflect normalized/standardized values or a leaky one-step setup — never reconciled to dollar errors.

Internally inconsistent numbers: the summary, DP section (3745.63% then '37456.33%'), and GA section ($442,697.25 with both 44,269.73% and 44,169.70% and 'golds 135.48%') disagree; figures (e.g., Fig 15 dates run into 2022/2023) contradict the 2016-2021 window.

The 'proof of optimality' is not a proof: beating buy-and-hold in three hand-picked regimes and beating one alternative stop-loss rule shows the strategy is good, not optimal; no bound, no exhaustive comparison, no statistical test.

Weak risk treatment: the assumption of an 'extreme risk-seeker' with no position control conveniently sidesteps drawdown/volatility metrics (no Sharpe, max drawdown), so the headline return ignores the path risk a real investor faces.

Gene3 (buying/breakout strategies) is described but never concretely operationalized in the shown code; several genes (frequency, breakout type) are not clearly wired into the actual MATLAB decision logic, so the GA may be doing less than the encoding implies.
The DP is really a greedy day-by-day rule (pick max ROA among BTC/gold/cash), not a true Bellman optimal-substructure DP — the 'dynamic programming' label oversells a greedy heuristic.
Transaction-cost sensitivity is only 4 discrete points (one summary figure with a single x=19 row), not a real sweep, so 'sensitivity' is more illustrative than quantitative.
Pervasive English/typo errors and broken figure captions (three figures all labeled 'Figure 16'), which a stricter judge would penalize.
reusable_patterns: Forecast -> baseline policy -> metaheuristic-optimized policy is a reusable three-stage template for any 'design a strategy from data' problem (trading, inventory, scheduling).
Justify a hybrid model by decomposing the signal (linear trend vs nonlinear residual) and benchmark hybrid vs each component in one RMSE/MAPE table — a compact, persuasive validation pattern.
Encode a complex decision policy as a chromosome of named, table-documented genes (Tables 2-5 style) so a GA can search heterogeneous choices; readers immediately grasp the search space.

Operationalize 'prove your solution is optimal/robust' along two orthogonal axes: perturb the SOLUTION (swap one design parameter and show degradation) and perturb the ENVIRONMENT (slice the data into regimes and show consistent outperformance vs a baseline).
Always benchmark against a naive baseline (here buy-and-hold of each asset) so the headline result has context.
Discount nominal terminal value to present using a stated risk-free rate to handle time-value-of-money in multi-year financial problems.
Turn a required 'letter/memo' subtask into a persona-driven, plain-English communication piece that restates assumptions and candidly lists limitations — judges reward honest, client-facing framing.
State the objective's hidden assumption explicitly (here: 'best' is undefined without a risk preference, so fix it to risk-seeker) to neutralize ambiguity in the problem statement.
Ship runnable code in the appendix as evidence of reproducibility, even if abbreviated.
